title: Product of Array Except Self
description:
Given an integer array `nums`, return an array `answer` such that `answer[i]` is the product of all elements of `nums` except `nums[i]`.

You must write an algorithm that runs in **O(n)** time without using the division operator.
constraints: The product of any prefix or suffix fits in a 32-bit integer

hint_plan.level_1: Each index needs the product of everything to the left and everything to the right.
hint_plan.level_2: Two passes: prefix products, then suffix products, combine without division.
hint_plan.level_3: First pass build prefix[i] = product of nums[0..i-1]; second pass multiply by running suffix from the right.
hint_plan.level_4: answer[i] = (product of nums before i) * (product of nums after i), computed in O(n) with two sweeps.

canonical_solution_summary: Prefix and suffix cumulative products in two passes; handle zeros carefully.
